question.text: Anita has a total income of Rs. 30,00,000 under the new tax regime for FY 2025-26. What is her income tax before surcharge and cess?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** New regime FY 2025-26 slabs on total income of Rs. 30,00,000: Up to 4L = Nil; 4-8L = 4L × 5% = Rs. 20,000; 8-12L = 4L × 10% = Rs. 40,000; 12-16L = 4L × 15% = Rs. 60,000; 16-20L = 4L × 20% = Rs. 80,000; 20-24L = 4L × 25% = Rs. 1,00,000; 24-30L = 6L × 30% = Rs. 1,80,000. Total = 20,000 + 40,000 + 60,000 + 80,000 + 1,00,000 + 1,80,000 = Rs. 4,80,000. **The Trap:** Rs. 5,40,000 results from applying flat 30% on Rs. 18L (above basic exemption of Rs. 12L under some mistaken logic). Rs. 4,20,000 is the tax on Rs. 28L — candidates may undercount the top slab. Rs. 5,10,000 results from using old regime slabs on part of the income. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-B extensively tests new regime computation for investment advisory planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** With the new regime's wider slabs in FY 2025-26, the effective rate on Rs. 30L is only 16% — significantly lower than the old regime. Always run both regime calculations for clients earning Rs. 15-30L.
